en.name: Albumin in blood
en.longDescription: ## Definition & Clinical Context Albumin is the main protein synthesised by the liver, accounting for ~60 % of total plasma protein. Its concentration in blood reflects the long-term nutritional and synthetic status, and is decreased in chronic liver disease, malnutrition, nephrotic syndrome, and systemic inflammation [1]. The source data will typically be a single numeric result per patient-timestamp, produced by a biochemistry analyser. Results are reported as a mass concentration in g/L (SI unit) or g/dL [2]. This concept set aggregates albumin measurements across blood specimens (serum, plasma and unspecified blood). ## Included Concepts The set is anchored on four LOINC Hierarchy concepts (one per blood specimen, scoped to `Chemistry - non-challenge`), with their descendants pulled in. After exclusions, the set resolves to 11 LOINC "Lab Test" concepts, grouped here by specimen. ### Serum or plasma (default specimen for routine biochemistry) - *3024561 / 1751-7 — Albumin [Mass/volume] in Serum or Plasma (LOINC)*: generic LOINC code, method-agnostic. Most widely used. - *3028286 / 2862-1 — Albumin [Mass/volume] in Serum or Plasma by Electrophoresis (LOINC)*: same measurand, with electrophoresis explicit. - *3965752 / 101198-0 — Albumin [Mass/volume] in Serum or Plasma by Nephelometry (LOINC)*: same measurand, with nephelometry explicit. - *40763912 / 61151-7 — Albumin [Mass/volume] in Serum or Plasma by Bromocresol green (BCG) dye binding method (LOINC)*: same measurand, with BCG dye method explicit. - *40763913 / 61152-5 — Albumin [Mass/volume] in Serum or Plasma by Bromocresol purple (BCP) dye binding method (LOINC)*: same measurand, with BCP dye method explicit. - *40757478 / 54347-0 — Albumin [Moles/volume] in Serum or Plasma (LOINC)*: substance concentration form. - *40764995 / 62234-0 — Albumin [Moles/volume] in Serum or Plasma by Bromocresol purple (BCP) dye binding method (LOINC)*: substance concentration form, BCP method. - *40764996 / 62235-7 — Albumin [Moles/volume] in Serum or Plasma by Bromocresol green (BCG) dye binding method (LOINC)*: substance concentration form, BCG method. ### Serum - *1260115 / 103577-3 — Albumin [Mass/volume] in Serum by Immunoassay (LOINC)* ### Generic blood (specimen unspecified) - *46235170 / 76631-1 — Albumin [Mass/volume] in Blood by Bromocresol purple (BCP) dye binding method (LOINC)* - *46236953 / 77148-5 — Albumin [Mass/volume] in Serum, Plasma or Blood by Bromocresol purple (BCP) dye binding method (LOINC)*: specimen-agnostic. **Note on units.** Recommended unit follows LOINC's `EXAMPLE_UCUM_UNITS`: **`g/L`** (concept 8636 — gram per litre) is the SI form. Conversion: `1 g/dL = 10 g/L`. The substance concentration form (`[Moles/volume]`) is reported in `µmol/L` — conversion via the molar mass of albumin (~66.5 kg/mol): `1 g/L ≈ 15 µmol/L`. **Note on BCG vs. BCP methods.** The bromocresol green (BCG) and bromocresol purple (BCP) dye-binding methods are the two most common laboratory assays for albumin, but they are **not numerically equivalent**: BCG tends to give slightly higher values than BCP, especially at low albumin concentrations, due to differential cross-reactivity with non-albumin plasma proteins. The LOINC code preserves the method when known. Study designs that compare albumin across centres should consider the assay method. ## Excluded Concepts The Serum/Plasma hierarchy above pulls in seven LOINC concepts that are explicitly excluded: ### Ratios — different measurand (ratio to another analyte) - *3020509 / 1759-0 — Albumin/Globulin [Mass Ratio] in Serum or Plasma* - *3046948 / 44429-9 — Albumin/Globulin [Mass Ratio] in Serum or Plasma by Electrophoresis* - *3007424 / 13980-8 — Albumin/Protein.total in Serum or Plasma by Electrophoresis* - *3035472 / 35706-1 — Albumin/Protein.total in Serum or Plasma* - *43055432 / 72598-6 — Albumin/Protein.total [Pure mass fraction] in Serum or Plasma by Electrophoresis* ### Qualitative — different measurand (presence, not a concentration) - *3043798 / 43712-9 — Albumin [Presence] in Serum or Plasma* ### Timed dialysis protocol — protocol-specific timepoint, out of scope - *3018721 / 21058-3 — Albumin [Mass/volume] in Serum or Plasma --2 hours post peritoneal dialysis* The set also deliberately omits the following LOINC concepts: - **Urine specimens** (e.g. microalbuminuria): captured by a separate concept set when relevant. - **CSF, body fluid, ascites, peritoneal fluid, pleural fluid and other specimens**: out of scope for a "blood" concept set. - **Fetus specimens**: out of scope. ## Mapping Notes - **Default concept** when the source reports "albumin", "Alb", "albuminémie" without further specification: `3024561 / 1751-7 — Albumin [Mass/volume] in Serum or Plasma`. By far the most common LOINC code. - **Source unit is g/dL**: convert to g/L by multiplying by 10. - **Source mentions BCG or BCP method**: map to the corresponding `61151-7` (BCG) or `61152-5` (BCP) code. The two are **not numerically equivalent** — preserve the source method when known. - **Source reports microalbuminuria**: do **not** map to this concept set. Urinary albumin is a different measurand, captured by a separate concept set when relevant. - **Source reports albumin/globulin ratio or albumin/total protein ratio**: do **not** map. These are different measurands. - **Do not confuse with**: urinary albumin (microalbuminuria), total protein, prealbumin (transthyretin, a different protein). ## References [1] Regenstrief Institute. LOINC code 1751-7 — Albumin [Mass/volume] in Serum or Plasma. Available: https://loinc.org/1751-7/ [2] Nomenclature for Properties and Units (NPU). IFCC/IUPAC. NPU codes (current release). Available: https://npu-terminology.org/npu-database/
